texto: We came out on Coldra Crescent, one of those warm air nights, stars spitting in the sky like firebugs stuck on a big black velvet rump. A summer night to make anybody with standard glands feel that tidy homes, spring mattresses, four guaranteed meals per day, and legalised religion were all criminal to human development.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Ron Berry: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a fe. O trecho “We came out on Coldra Crescent, one of those warm air nights, stars spitting in the sky like fire…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, summer, summer-nights pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.
